submission_xml.group_contribution:
I contributed to several tasks within the group project. My role involved helping to organise the dataset structure, discussing the system design with teammates, and assisting with the implementation of the XML data format. I also collaborated with the group to review the schema design and ensure that the data could be validated correctly. Additionally, I helped test the XML files and checked that the structure followed the schema requirements.
I contributed to the group project by refactoring the front-end of the application to make it responsive across different screen sizes. I applied a CSS framework to improve the layout, styling, and overall user experience of the website. This included restructuring the page layout, improving navigation, and ensuring that the interface worked well on both desktop and mobile devices. I supported other group members by reviewing parts of the implementation and assisting with tasks where needed to ensure the project progressed smoothly.

individual_component.type: Refactor the data set using XML and XML-Schema
individual_component.description:
My individual task was to refactor the dataset into XML format and design an appropriate XML Schema (XSD) to validate the structure. This involved analysing the original dataset, converting the data into well-structured XML elements, and ensuring that the hierarchy and relationships between data fields were logical and consistent. The schema was used to enforce rules such as required elements, correct data types, and valid structure.
My individual task was to refactor the front-end of the application to make it responsive and improve the overall user interface. I applied a CSS framework to enhance the design, layout, and usability of the website. This involved restructuring the page layout, improving the navigation, and ensuring that the website adapts correctly to different screen sizes such as desktops, tablets, and mobile devices. I also used additional tools such as icon libraries to improve the visual appearance of the application.
individual_component.problems:
During development, several issues were encountered. One problem was ensuring that the XML structure matched the schema exactly, as even small mistakes could cause validation errors. Another challenge was deciding the best way to organise the data hierarchy so that it remained readable and scalable. Additionally, some validation errors occurred when data types or required elements were missing.
One of the main challenges was adapting the existing layout so that it worked correctly on different screen sizes. Some elements did not align properly when viewed on smaller devices, and certain components needed to be reorganised using responsive grid systems. Another issue was ensuring that styling from the new CSS framework did not conflict with the existing styles in the project.
individual_component.solutions:
These problems were solved by carefully reviewing the XML schema and validating the document regularly using an XML validation tool. The structure of the XML file was improved by reorganising elements into a clearer hierarchy. Validation errors were fixed by ensuring that all required elements were included and that the correct data types were used according to the schema rules.
These problems were solved by restructuring the layout using the responsive grid system provided by the CSS framework. Media queries and responsive classes were applied to adjust the layout depending on the screen size. Conflicting styles were resolved by reviewing the CSS structure and applying consistent styling rules across the application.

file.name: index.html
file.purpose: Main page where Bootstrap classes were applied to create a responsive layout

file.name: style.css
file.purpose: Custom styling used alongside the CSS framework to improve the design

submission_xml.security_discussion:
Security considerations were important when handling external data sources and web-based technologies. Proper validation of XML files was necessary to prevent malformed data from being processed. Additionally, input validation and sanitisation should be implemented in server-side scripts to reduce risks such as injection attacks. Access permissions and secure data handling practices are also important to ensure that sensitive information is protected and that the system operates safely.
Data security is an important consideration when developing web applications. The Parkerian Hexad identifies six attributes that define information security. In the development of our group application, several of these attributes were considered, particularly in relation to the refactoring of the front-end to create a responsive and accessible user interface.
Confidentiality refers to protecting information from unauthorised access. While the responsive front-end mainly focuses on user interface design, it still plays a role in protecting sensitive information by ensuring that data is displayed appropriately across different devices. Responsive layouts help prevent sensitive content from being accidentally exposed or displayed incorrectly on smaller screens, which could otherwise make information easier to view by unintended users.
Integrity ensures that information remains accurate and is not modified in an unauthorised way. In the application, integrity can be supported through proper input validation and structured data handling. When refactoring the front-end, form elements and user inputs were organised using consistent layout structures, helping to ensure that data entered by users follows the expected format before being processed by the system.
Authenticity refers to verifying that users and data sources are genuine. Although authentication mechanisms are typically handled on the server side, the front-end still contributes by clearly presenting login forms, navigation, and user interface elements that help users interact with the system correctly. A well-structured and responsive interface reduces the risk of user confusion and helps ensure that users interact with legitimate parts of the application.
Availability is another important attribute of the Parkerian Hexad. By refactoring the front-end to be responsive, the application becomes accessible across a wide range of devices, including mobile phones, tablets, and desktop computers. This improves availability because users can access and use the system regardless of the device they are using. Responsive design also improves performance and usability, ensuring that the application loads correctly and remains functional across different screen sizes.
By considering these aspects of the Parkerian Hexad during development, the application becomes more secure, reliable, and accessible to users across multiple platforms.
submission_xml.individual_comment:
This section of the project was completed individually. The task helped improve my understanding of XML structure, schema validation, and how data can be organised in a structured and machine-readable format. Through this work, I gained practical experience in validating XML files and ensuring that they conform to defined schema rules.
This part of the project was completed individually and focused on refactoring the front-end of the application to make it responsive and improve the overall user interface. Through this task, I developed a better understanding of responsive web design and how CSS frameworks can be used to create flexible layouts that adapt to different screen sizes. I also gained practical experience in improving website usability, organising page structure, and ensuring that the application provides a consistent user experience across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
